summary:
Claim: The contractile part of intrafusal fibers is primarily located at their ends.  
Evidence: Histological studies confirm contractile elements align peripherally, near sensory receptors; end-specific contractions drive detectable displacement of sensory endings during stretch.  
Answer: B
